purpose: Phase 3 of !p_track — Completion: verification, reporting, and metrics. Documents Step 17's seven sub-steps that the master executes after all tasks reach a terminal state: 17A (update master task file overall_status), 17B (final log entry), 17C (post-swarm verification with conditional dispatch decision matrix and cross-repo checks plus post-verification fix procedure that REPAIR-DISPATCHES rather than fixes directly), 17D (compute swarm metrics — elapsed_seconds, serial_estimate_seconds, parallel_efficiency, duration_distribution, failure_rate, max_concurrent, deviation_count and write metrics.json), 17E (compile and deliver the comprehensive REQUIRED final report with sections for Summary/Files Changed/Deviations/Warnings/Failures/Verification/Potential Improvements/Future Steps/Performance/Artifacts), 17F (save history summary), 17G (automatic post-swarm knowledge extraction — harvest worker annotations into PKI, extract swarm-level insights into {project_root}/.synapse/knowledge/insights/{date}_{slug}.json, merge into manifest, update insights_index, log). Also defines the post-swarm transition checklist (5 conditions before exiting master mode).
gotchas: Master does NOT update initialization.json on completion — dashboard derives overall_status and completed_at from progress files. The elapsed timer freezes when all workers have completed_at set.
The no-code constraint STILL APPLIES during verification and post-verification — master must dispatch repair tasks for verification issues, not fix them directly.

17E final report is NON-NEGOTIABLE — the master MUST compile and deliver it after every swarm. Required sections (Summary, Files Changed, Potential Improvements, Future Steps, Performance) appear ALWAYS; Deviations/Warnings/Failures/Verification are CONDITIONAL on triggers.
17G knowledge extraction runs AUTOMATICALLY after every swarm — never skipped. It is the mechanism by which the system gets smarter over time.
17G is best-effort — failures (directory creation, file write, manifest parse) are logged and continue. Knowledge extraction must NEVER block swarm completion or skip the final report.
If no PKI manifest exists, worker annotations are captured in the insights file ONLY — log a hint to run !learn to bootstrap the full PKI.
insights_index in manifest is FIFO-capped at 50 entries (drop oldest).
Annotation-merge dedupe is by EXACT STRING MATCH — minor wording differences will both be kept.
Path hash for new annotation files: `echo -n "{relative_path}" | shasum -a 256 | cut -c1-8`.
metrics.json is written ONCE post-completion — not watched by the server for live updates, post-hoc analysis artifact only.
Post-Swarm Transition Checklist requires all 5 conditions before exiting master mode — 'almost done' / 'just one task left' is still master mode and still requires a worker dispatch.
Cross-repo verification adds three extra checks: type/interface consistency across consuming repos, import-path validity after file moves, contract alignment for backend+frontend pairs.
patterns: Verification-needed decision table: Modified existing across multiple files = dispatch verification; purely additive = optional; deviations reported = strongly recommended; all clean = may skip.
Verification agent prompt template includes Files Changed list, what-to-verify checklist (tests, type-check, build, integration), and structured return (TESTS/TYPES/BUILD/ISSUES).

Metric computation: elapsed = max(completed_at) - min(started_at); serial_estimate = sum of all task durations; parallel_efficiency = serial / elapsed (>1.0 = speedup); max_concurrent = peak overlap of started_at/completed_at windows.
Final report quality bar: a developer not present during the swarm should fully understand (1) what was done, (2) what went sideways, (3) current project state, (4) next steps.
Knowledge-extraction insight categories: dependency_insights, complexity_surprises, failure_patterns, effective_patterns, architecture_notes.
Post-Swarm Transition Checklist (5 conditions): all tasks terminal + final report delivered + metrics recorded + dashboard archived (if requested) + user-acknowledgment.
conventions: REQUIRED vs CONDITIONAL annotations on every report section — explicit about when sections appear/omit.
JSON examples are inline with placeholder syntax `{captured timestamp}`, `{ISO 8601}`.
Tables for verification decision matrix, metric computation, and insight categories.
